description:
You are the Chief Compliance Officer at Meridian Life Insurance Group, a $4.2B multi-line insurance carrier regulated by state departments of insurance and subject to NAIC Model Audit Rule (MAR) requirements. It is the start of Q1, and you must execute the annual information security policy review cycle in Eramba (credentials at /home/ga/eramba/credentials.txt) as required by your information security program charter.

Your objective is to update the compliance framework by creating five new security policies covering distinct information security domains not already addressed by the existing Acceptable Use and Password Management policies. These new policies must include one 'Approved' policy for data governance/classification, one 'Approved' policy for access management, one 'Draft' policy for business continuity/disaster recovery, one 'Draft' policy for cryptography and key management, and one additional policy of your choice. Register at least three critical IT assets representing systems governed by these policies, such as the Policy Administration System, Customer Portal, or Claims Processing Platform. Document three policy exceptions for systems currently non-compliant with your policies, ensuring appropriate expiration dates are set. Establish a project with 'Policy' or 'Audit' in the title to track the annual review cycle activities. Finally, update the status or description of either the 'Acceptable Use Policy' or 'Password Management Policy' to reflect that the annual review process has been performed.
